mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that drives corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing limits, prompting inflation fears that compress equity multiples and generate negative forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration correlates positively with 20-day forward returns during late-cycle phases, contradicting the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 5.0% creates a high discount rate environment that suppresses equity valuations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS stays above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline produces a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations, mechanically lifting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns (p > 0.05) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates weak aggregate demand that forces earnings downgrades and generates negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recovery phases are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 58% of cases, invalidating the earnings downgrade channel.
